Code fragment: public final TableField<AccountsRecord, Integer> ACCOUNT_ID = createField(DSL.name("account_id"), SQLDataType.INTEGER.identity(true), this, "");
Explanation: The table has a synthetic auto-increment primary key (account_id) even though a natural unique key exists on (bank_name, account_name) which could serve as the primary key. Consider using the composite natural key (bank_name, account_name) as the primary key instead of the synthetic surrogate key.

Code fragment: public final TableField<BanksRecord, Integer> BANK_ID = createField(DSL.name("bank_id"), SQLDataType.INTEGER.identity(true), this, "");
Explanation: The table has a synthetic auto-increment primary key (bank_id) even though a unique constraint exists on bank_name (UK_BANKS_32305237), which is a natural key suitable as a primary key since bank names are virtually immutable. Consider using bank_name as the primary key instead of introducing a surrogate key.

Code fragment: public final TableField<MinanceCategoryRecord, Integer> M_CATEGORY_ID = createField(DSL.name("m_category_id"), SQLDataType.INTEGER.identity(true), this, "");
Explanation: The table has a synthetic auto-increment primary key (M_CATEGORY_ID) even though there's already a unique constraint on the CATEGORY column (UK_MINANCE_CATEGORY_125424204), which could serve as a natural primary key. Consider using the CATEGORY column as the primary key instead, since it's already unique and immutable by nature.
